description: A capabilities document whose optional 'limits.idempotency_key_ttl_seconds' field is JSON null. acdp-capabilities.schema.json types it as an integer ("type": "integer", range 86400..604800), NOT as a nullable type. Per the absent-vs-null wire convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1), a registry that does not advertise idempotency-key support MUST OMIT 'idempotency_key_ttl_seconds', never serialize it as null. A consumer validating a capabilities document (validate_capabilities, RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.5) MUST reject 'idempotency_key_ttl_seconds': null as schema_violation. (When the registry DOES advertise supports_idempotency_key: true, the field is required AND non-null — null fails for both reasons; the absent-vs-null violation is independent of the supports_idempotency_key value.)

expected.rationale: idempotency_key_ttl_seconds is an optional, non-nullable integer. A registry not honoring Idempotency-Key omits the key entirely rather than emitting null. A consumer that coerces null to absent special-cases the wire form; a strict consumer rejects the document as a schema_violation per the absent-vs-null convention.
